title: The Journey So Far Establishing My Automated Trading System
author: Yinnebecivil
selftext:
I go by the name Yinne and I currently reside in the UK.

A short story of mine is that I started working on an algorithmic trading system a couple of years ago, mainly out of frustration with how inconsistent manual trading felt.

I expected the hard part to be building profitable strategies. Turns out, that’s not even the biggest challenge.

A few things that woke me up:

\- Slippage and fees matter way more than I thought.

\- Risk management has a bigger impact than entry signals.

\- The effect of compounding is a lot more meaningful than I initially gave it credit for, especially compared to chasing high short-term returns

\- Capital preservation sounds boring, but once you go through drawdowns, you realise how important steady progress actually is

At this stage, the system is holding up in live conditions to a standard, especially given how volatile markets have been recently. It’s taken a lot of iteration and adjustment to get to something that feels relatively stable.

Definitely still a work in progress, but getting to this point has been a bit of a turning point after a lot of trial and error.

Curious to hear from others.. What has been your experience been like with algo trading systems?

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: Dude, your insights on slippage and fees being the real MVPs over strategy are genuinely mindblowing, like how did you even crack that code?

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body: It is ignored and treated as the least important thing, however, trading in volatile markets you have to become aware of such costs as they impact your trading balance and overtime it adds up.

comments.author: d_e_g_m
comments.body: This place would be a better place if instead of rising pnl charts, we would discuss what strategy works and what doesnt with real data, the math and hard cold results. To those of you who present that kind of work, I thank you and salute you. You are the MVPs

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body: Then you might miss opportunity and good exchanges from your judgement.

replies.author: CrazyButRightOn
replies.body: Just post your strategy if you really want to help people

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
I have shared MyFxbook which is public. In that performance data there is enough detailed stats to build a Thesis on my system. 

Do you have previous experience with algorithmic trading systems?

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
You can look at hard cold results by asking me for an Fxbook then I would share it for you to see these finer details. I understand your frustration, however not every individual that posts is the same, it’s about engaging in that conversation and asking questions. Negative assumptions does not start the discussion off well.

[FX QUANT ALGO](https://www.myfxbook.com/members/fxenvy/fluxforex-quant-algo--e2/11948627)

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: honestly the transition from "finding the perfect entry" to focusing on capital preservation is the biggest hurdle. i’ve had a similar journey and it’s why i stopped trying to manually hunt for every setup. lately i just use signalwhisper to handle the initial signal filtering. it gives me a clear technical starting point so i can spend 100% of my energy on the risk management and position sizing side, which like you said, is where the actual money is made.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
I’ve built an ML trading system for BTC and Gold that I’ve been working on for a while. It’s been backtested over 5 years and has averaged around 10% per month on fixed lot, with Sharpe above 3.

I’ve also been live trading it since September, and what I’m most proud of is that the model stayed positive through the entire spot drawdown.

I don’t currently have the capital to scale it myself, so I’m open to speaking with someone serious who may be interested in acquiring it.

Full backtest report here: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1xwNisxVslkfPPY9g2rcbWt4shrI-9o4-/view?usp=drive_link)

If it looks relevant, happy to have a straightforward conversation and share live trade data as well.

comments.author: serious84
comments.body: so you highest balance was lower than your profit? make it make sense :D

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body: You had the opportunity before you left a comment to evaluate or even understand what that data entails. If you was intrigued about what highest balance stands for, I don’t mind you asking as from my understanding you didn’t quite grasp what the data suggests.

comments.body: is it profitable in longer term?

replies.body:
Hello! As you can see from the very beginning it has been growing on an upwards trajectory through the chart. I’ll share the fx book link below so you can see for yourself

comments.author: celzo1776
comments.body: I find it laughable that 99% of the posts here belive they have found a unique way of predicting the market

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body: Afternoon! Do you know what MyFxbook is?

comments.author: SlechteConcentratie
comments.body: Is your computer a beast or do you trade low frequency?

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
Hahaha! The two biggest factors that’s paved the way is honing compound growth & capital preservation. 

No need for fast high returns as that comes with heavy baggage, and that’s too much risk.

comments.author: MountainFancy1352
comments.body: How long did you back test/ paper trade before you went live? I'm starting a test run of a claude agent Monday on IBKR, I need to finish hooking up to github and thats it. Claude tells me several months but I want it quicker lol

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
Months is not enough from personal experience. It took years for this to be at this stage, however, who is to say what you have got going on is efficient and you could get it to a level In a more compressed timeline. You will find out for yourself. 

This went through multiple vendors.. 4 figure capital, 5 figure. Prop firms, personal capital, 6 figures on paper trading. Claude has gave you hope 😆🫰

replies.author: MountainFancy1352
replies.body: Actually my model  will not backtest, it will paper trade forward based on 10 different math probabilistic algorithyms, but you are right, I should let it run its course, as it tells me that the operator ( me) is the no 1 cause of failure. I believe it.

comments.author: RedStar1996
comments.body: In not asking for details on your strategy but I’m just wondering what you use for it? Technical indicators? Price action?

replies.author: RedStar1996
replies.body: Based off what? How did you come up with it?

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body: To go into context execution and risk management is very important. Coming up with it was years of studying. Experimenting and having the manual trading experience was the eye opener. What is the next thing that could take trading to the next level… automated trading!

comments.author: ChoppyTrader
comments.body: It’s very difficult to draw conclusions about algorithms without seeing the closed trades and holding periods. But in my experience, if the chart is rising without any pullbacks, there is massive loss carrying. Which, when combined with a strong counter-trend movement, can easily lead to a margin call or result in losing trades piling up for a very long time, turning into unmanageable risk.

replies.author: Yinnebecivil
replies.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. Your talking point is definitely one for discussion & valid.

So far there has been no margin calls from all investor accounts running under us. The bot has seen drawdown at times however what still impresses me is the algorithm system. For example if you was to put a human to interact with situation’s like that, there would be a lot of emotions which could lead to further problems for the account/s. At times it’s added further positions while in drawdown and either left with small profit, break even or minimal loss. The adaptive nature to the system gives it a great edge and to one that’s not stagnant and cannot act in the face of loss. 

I can recall in late January it faced BTC pair losses and the way it manoeuvred to bring the losses to a minimum was class in its self.  As to your point on carrying losses, the system uses micro lots so expecting margin calls is highly unlikely. Further more we give opportunity to bespoke clients whom chose to have a bigger risk appetite and fit their goals so it’s not limited. 

Being able to tweak the system is something that is prioritised as market conditions can change, so I can never play ignorant to your points. At the moment it is in a great position and I’m doing everything to make sure we manage it accordingly. 

Thank you again!
